title: christella parasitica
intro: Christella parasitica, also known as the parasitic fern or the Christ's fern, is a species of fern that grows as an epiphyte on other plants, deriving its nutrients by parasitizing the host plant. The medicinal properties of Christella parasitica lie in its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antiseptic, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of using Christella parasitica include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. Christella parasitica is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the leaves being used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
